title: Post Zeruel repairs
wikitext:
[]: Post-Zeruel repairs             Quote Post by AyrYntake » Sat Nov 22, 2008 5:15 pm As described in the topic. It just seems pretty implausible to me that with an uncontrollable, godlike Yui-sama to deal with, NERV had the time to make repairs to a mutilated, beheaded Kyoko and a Unit-00 with a head split in half...and even if you argue that there was a time frame of 2 episodes between the fourteenth and fifteenth Angels, HOW DID THEY DO IT?? I mean, how do you take the head and both arms of a 40-meter-tall (at LEAST) humanoid and fit its head back on seamlessly while it is, for all intents and purposes, not functioning? After all, Kyoko's soul being dormant at the time, she couldn't have regenerated the same way Yui-sama did earlier, could she? Unless there's some method NERV have of inducing berserker modes in Eva Units at will, which I highly doubt, I don't see how these "repairs", which we only see cursorily in one episode post-Zeruel, can be carried out. Somebody please enlighten me on this.

[]:             Quote Post by Reichu » Sat Nov 22, 2008 5:21 pm Check this string of posts out .

[]:             Quote Post by AyrYntake » Sat Nov 22, 2008 5:35 pm Hmm? All I got from that was "they're messing around with cellular processes directly"...I'm not sure what that entails.

[]:             Quote Post by jim_loves_randy » Sat Nov 22, 2008 5:53 pm I guess since unit 02 is a production model, repairs would be easier and faster? Also, Nerv is a super high tech advanced efficient government facility with lots of funds and resourses so I guess they showed their potential to handle all that work in a short amount of time.

[]:             Quote Post by NemZ » Sat Nov 22, 2008 7:11 pm Well, they had a whole month to do it in and have previously shown some pretty advanced ability to repair these suckers on a tight clock. They also scrapped the in-progress 05 and 06 production models for spare parts.

[]:             Quote Post by V » Sat Nov 22, 2008 7:49 pm indeed; off-hand they mention that the original (and incomplete) Evas 05 and 06 were scrapped for "parts" to repair Eva 00 and 02. Tissue transplants? replace the arms? I dunno; they had to fix Eva-02's original head, but yeah; there *is* a point at which an Eva is so damaged they cannot repair it, and I think they surpassed that in episode 19 actually (the Heinflick limit or something) so they had to go the extra mile and use "replacement parts" (transplants) from incomplete Evas to fix them. and they had a whole month

[]:             Quote Post by Reichu » Sat Nov 22, 2008 8:22 pm They might have needed to give Eva-00 a completely new head (considering it was bisected and all), but it looks like they just reattached 02's original head and arms. They were sliced clean off, so why not? Needed to attach a head and arms one way or the other. @Ayr: The stuff I linked to talked about Nerv taking control of the Evas' innate regeneration mechanisms in order to "repair" them.

[]:             Quote Post by NemZ » Sat Nov 22, 2008 9:03 pm Random speculation... if 03 and 04 were based on the same genotype, what if 05 and 06 are actually clones of 02 and 00? Explains the spare parts thing pretty easily that way.

[]:             Quote Post by Reichu » Sat Nov 22, 2008 9:26 pm Why would they want to base a new model on the shitty prototype? A direct successor to Eva-02 is the far more likely of the two. But, like I said, reattaching 02's severed head and arms amounts to pretty much the same thing as using the severed head and arms of an Eva nobody wanted, so I really doubt the "spare parts" came into the picture with 02's repair.

[]:             Quote Post by NemZ » Sat Nov 22, 2008 10:30 pm The problem with the prototype is in the core, not the basic genome. After the refit 00 should only be inferior to 02 because of operational issues arising from the mysterious weirdness of its resident soul... they seem physically identical other than color and head design. The shoulder pylons may contain different gear for each eva but there's no reason to suggest their payloads can't be switched out if desired. You didn't honestly think that the "00 isn't compatible with the D-type armor" line was anything but an excuse to make sure the designated fodder pilot took the suicide mission, did you? Asuka is to NERV what Chef was to Cartman's hippy drill. If the spares didn't come into play, why are they mentioned at all?

[]:             Quote Post by Reichu » Sat Nov 22, 2008 11:26 pm If the spares didn't come into play, why are they mentioned at all?

[]:             Quote Post by V » Sat Nov 22, 2008 11:26 pm NemZ wrote: If the spares didn't come into play, why are they mentioned at all?

[]:             Quote Post by NemZ » Sun Nov 23, 2008 2:31 pm Hmm. I took that to mean Misato was wondering more about shy there was such a big push now, and why they were being sent parts if the production was being stepped up. In retrospect I thought that Seele was scrapping the production models in favor of developing the new harpy model, hopefully giving Gendo a false sense of security when new production was held up by repair requisitions.

[]:             Quote Post by Reichu » Sun Nov 23, 2008 3:34 pm NemZ wrote: In retrospect I thought that Seele was scrapping the production models in favor of developing the new harpy model , hopefully giving Gendo a false sense of security when new production was held up by repair requisitions.
summary: []: Post-Zeruel repairs             Quote Post by AyrYntake » Sat Nov 22, 2008 5:15 pm As described in the topic. It just seems pretty implausible to me that with an uncontrollable, godlike Yui-sama to deal with, NERV had the time to make repairs to a mutilated, beheaded Kyoko and a Unit-00 with a head split in half...and even if you argue that there was a time frame of 2 episodes between the fourteenth and fifteenth Angels, HOW DID THEY DO
